title: Specklinia minor
taxonomy: Specklinia minor is classified in the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places Specklinia minor within the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Cranichideae. The species Specklinia minor is further divided into various subspecies based on specific characteristics. This detailed classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and identification of the species.

intro: Specklinia minor, also known as the Small Speckled Orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and promoting immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Specklinia minor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Specklinia minor include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and fever in traditional medicine.
